description: Phase-1 parity declarations for the Python client and planned TypeScript peer.

capabilities.reason: The Development-owned TS adapter adds a bounded public error_category enum; the Python package exposes sanitized error text in its internal runtime receipt and does not own that adapter taxonomy.

not_yet_extracted.scenario: fallback-runner command and receipt ordering matrix
not_yet_extracted.reason: The current assertions mix transport fallback mechanics with scope proof; phase 1 keeps those tests authoritative instead of inventing a runtime-neutral fallback command contract.

not_yet_extracted.scenario: corrupt JSONL, fsync failure, short-write, symlink, and concurrent-append matrix
not_yet_extracted.reason: These are Python JsonlSpool implementation and filesystem durability details, not yet a matched-client wire contract.

not_yet_extracted.scenario: disclosure-bundle rendering and reserved-field rejection matrix
not_yet_extracted.reason: The golden disclosure fixture is a separate export contract; its existing tests remain authoritative until the TS client owns an equivalent export surface.
